methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Silicon Quantum Computing, led by founder Michelle Simmons, is a Series A quantum computing company that has raised A$83M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises a fragmented peer universe of 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Standard peer-median valuation is not applicable here. The quantum computing peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables—only 1 adjacent-stage peer was identified against a requirement of at least 3 datapoints for statistical reliability. Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial distortion given the heterogeneity of business models, technical approaches, and capital efficiency across quantum hardware platforms, software stacks, and hybrid services providers.

Without a sufficiently dense comparable set at Series A in quantum computing, triangulated valuation synthesis cannot be supported from peer multiples alone. Valuation assessment would require supplementary methods—such as discounted cash flow under defined technical milestones, venture capital method calibration to exit assumptions and probability weighting, or transaction multiples from later-stage raises within the target's own funding lineage—none of which are present in the current dataset. The key uncertainty remains the extreme early-stage heterogeneity of quantum technology pathways and commercial timelines, which limits the relevance of even later-stage peer outcomes.
